questions.stem: Find the error: The company (A) / is in (B) / the red (C) / for three years. (D)
options: D - should be 'has been in the red for three years'
questions.solution: D - should be 'has been in the red for three years'

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Routing — operation sheet:
options: Detailed instructions for each operation: machine, tools, jigs, time
questions.solution: Operation sheet: sequence of operations. Route sheet: path through departments.

questions.subject: Industrial Engineering
questions.stem: Parallel (redundant) system: system fails when:
questions.solution: Parallel: only fails if all paths fail. Rs = 1 - (1-R₁)(1-R₂)... Much more reliable than series.

questions.subject: Industrial Engineering
questions.stem: Network flow max-flow min-cut theorem:
options: Maximum flow equals minimum cut capacity

questions.subject: Industrial Engineering
questions.stem: Demand planning accuracy measured by:
options: Forecast error metrics (MAPE, bias, tracking signal)
questions.solution: Forecast accuracy: lower error = better planning.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Sampling plans — MIL-STD-1916:
options: Replaced MIL-STD-105E. K (continuous sampling), L (lotted), M (attributes), N (variables). AQL based
questions.solution: MIL-STD-1916: preference for process control over acceptance sampling. Zero defect plans.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Systematic Layout Planning (SLP) relationship chart: A=absolutely necessary, E=especially important, I=important, O=ordinary, U=unimportant, X=undesirable
options: 6 closeness ratings from A to X
questions.solution: SLP: Muther. Relationship chart (6×6 matrix). Closeness ratings determine adjacency.

questions.subject: Industrial Engineering
questions.stem: Acceptance Sampling: AQL represents:
questions.solution: AQL: worst tolerable quality level. Sampling plans designed so that good lots have high probability of acceptance.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A PERT activity has to = 2 and tp = 8 days. Its standard deviation is:

questions.subject: Industrial Engineering
questions.stem: Finger force capability decreases when:
options: Wrist is deviated (flexed, extended, or ulnar/radial)
questions.solution: Deviation from neutral wrist reduces finger force. Combined posture + force = higher injury risk. Key ergonomic principle.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Forecast combination — simple average:
options: Combining ≥2 forecasts often beats individual. Average, weighted (by inverse MSE), or trimmed
questions.solution: Combination: reduces variance. Bates-Granger (1969). Weighted: w_i = (1/MSE_i)/Σ(1/MSE_j).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: An assembly line has a cycle time of 2 min and runs for 420 min/shift. The shift output is:
questions.solution: Output = available time / cycle time = 420/2 = 210 units.

questions.subject: Industrial Engineering
questions.stem: Earned Value Management: CPI = EV/AC. CPI < 1 means:
questions.solution: CPI < 1: spending more than planned for work completed. Cost overrun. CPI > 1: under budget.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Order picking methods:
options: Discrete, batch, zone, wave picking. Pick-to-light, voice, RF scanning
questions.solution: Discrete: one order at a time. Batch: multiple orders. Zone: picker assigned to zone.

questions.subject: Industrial Engineering
questions.stem: Learning curve effect: unit cost decreases by:
options: Fixed percentage for each doubling of cumulative output
questions.solution: Learning curve: Y = aX^b. Cost decreases by (100-L)% each time cumulative output doubles. L = learning rate.

questions.subject: Industrial Engineering
questions.stem: Queuing system M/M/1 average number in system L =

questions.subject: Industrial Engineering
questions.stem: Economic Order Quantity balances:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Lean Six Sigma — D.M.A.I.C. vs. D.F.S.S.:
options: DMAIC for existing process improvement. DFSS (DMADV) for new process design. 5 phases each
questions.solution: DFSS: Design For Six Sigma. IDDOV (Identify, Define, Design, Optimize, Validate). Transfer function y = f(x).

questions.subject: Industrial Engineering
questions.stem: SMED (Single-Minute Exchange of Die): goal is to:
options: Reduce setup/changeover time to under 10 minutes
questions.solution: SMED: Shingo. Separate internal (machine stop) from external (running) setup. Convert internal to external. Streamline.

questions.subject: Industrial Engineering
questions.stem: Design for Manufacturability (DFM) goals:
options: Reduce manufacturing cost while maintaining quality
questions.solution: DFM: minimize part count, standardize parts, simplify operations, reduce tolerances. Cross-functional collaboration.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Break-even: Fixed cost=$25000, variable cost=$25/unit, selling price=$40/unit. BEP≈

questions.subject: Industrial Engineering
questions.stem: Decision tree analysis uses:
options: Expected monetary value at chance nodes
questions.solution: EMV: Σ(probability × payoff). Decision nodes: choose max EMV. Chance nodes: compute weighted average. Incorporates probabilities.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Weibull distribution: R(t) = e^{-(t/η)^β}. For β < 1:
options: Failure rate decreases with time (infant mortality)
questions.solution: β<1: early failures (burn-in). β=1: constant failure rate (exponential). β>1: wear-out.

questions.subject: Industrial Engineering
questions.stem: Lead time offset in MRP:
options: Offsets requirements by lead time to get order release date
questions.solution: Offset: when to release order so it arrives when needed. Net requirement → planned order release = requirement date − lead time.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: MOST (Maynard Operation Sequence Technique):
options: Sequence models: General Move, Controlled Move, Tool Use. Higher level than MTM
questions.solution: MOST: 3 sequence models. General: A B G A B P A. Controlled: A B G A B X I A. Time in TMU.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Acceptance sampling:
options: Inspect sample from lot → accept/reject. Single, double, multiple, sequential plans
questions.solution: Sampling: less costly. Sampling risk: α (producer's risk), β (consumer's risk).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Ergonomics — REBA (Rapid Entire Body Assessment):
options: Scores for trunk, neck, legs, upper arm, lower arm, wrist. Coupling + activity. Score > 8 = high risk
questions.solution: REBA: Hignett & McAtamney (2000). Score A (trunk/neck/legs) + score B (arm/wrist) + load + activity.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Function analysis (FAST diagram):
options: How-Why logic. Basic vs secondary functions. All functions cost
questions.solution: FAST: Function Analysis System Technique. Critical path of functions. Value = Worth/Cost.

questions.subject: Industrial Engineering
questions.stem: Standard Work documents include:
options: Work sequence, takt time, standard WIP
questions.solution: Standard Work: best known method. Three elements: work sequence, takt time, standard WIP. Foundation for kaizen.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Facility layout: from-to chart measures:
options: Material flow between departments (trips, cost, or weight)
questions.solution: From-to chart: flow matrix. High flow → departments should be close. Used in SLP.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Shop floor control — DBR:
options: Drum (bottleneck schedule), Buffer (time buffer), Rope (release signal)
questions.solution: DBR: Goldratt (TOC). Bottleneck determines throughput. 5 focusing steps. S-DBR for MTO.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Reliability — standby redundancy:
options: Backup unit inactive until primary fails. Cold, warm, hot standby
questions.solution: Cold: zero failure rate when off. Warm: reduced failure rate. Hot: same failure rate. Perfect switching.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A PERT activity has to = 2 and tp = 14 days. Its standard deviation is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Forecasting types:
options: Qualitative (judgment, Delphi) and Quantitative (time series, causal)
questions.solution: Quantitative: needs historical data. Qualitative: when data is scarce.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: On the Pareto chart of defect causes shown (tallest bar = cause A), the principle being applied says you should first address:
options: Cause A (the tallest bar — the vital few)
questions.solution: The Pareto (80/20) principle prioritizes the 'vital few' tallest bars; cause A contributes the most defects and is tackled first.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Process capability — Pp/Ppk:
options: For initial process (pre-control). Pp = (USL-LSL)/(6σ_pp). Ppk = min(CPU, CPL) using overall σ
questions.solution: Ppk: based on overall standard deviation (long-term). Cp/Cpk: based on within-subgroup σ (short-term).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Process: μ=10, σ=0.5, USL=12, LSL=8. Cpk=

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Which statement about Cp and Cpk is correct?
options: Cpk is always greater than Cp
Cpk ≤ Cp, with equality only when the process is perfectly centred
questions.solution: Cpk penalizes off-centre processes, so Cpk ≤ Cp; they are equal only at perfect centring.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Kaizen: continuous improvement philosophy:
options: Small, incremental improvements by everyone in the organization
questions.solution: Kaizen: everyone participates. Daily improvement. 5S, waste elimination, standard work.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Forecasting — naive method:
options: Forecast = last observation. Simple benchmark. F_t = A_{t-1}
questions.solution: Naive: random walk (drift model). Seasonal naive: F_t = A_{t-s}. Benchmark for accuracy.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: RULA (Rapid Upper Limb Assessment):
options: Posture, force, repetition of upper body. Score 1-7. <3: acceptable, >7: investigate
questions.solution: RULA: for office/light tasks. REBA: for whole body/load handling. NIOSH lifting equation.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Material Requirement Planning (MRP):
options: Computes dependent demand. BOM + MPS + inventory → purchase/production orders
questions.solution: MRP: gross → net requirements. Lot sizing. MRPII: closed loop with capacity.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Vogel's Approximation Method (VAM) penalty:
options: Difference between two smallest costs in each row/column
questions.solution: VAM: penalty = |smallest - second smallest|. Higher penalty → more urgent to allocate smallest cost cell.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Process chart symbols:
questions.solution: Operation: value-adding. Inspection: quality check. Transport: movement.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: NPV: Initial=$25000, annual=$7000, r=8%, n=8yr. NPV=

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: DMAIC — Measure — data types:
options: Continuous (variable: dimension, time) vs discrete (attribute: pass/fail, count)
questions.solution: Continuous: measurement (gauge). Discrete: classification. Success rate, defect rate.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: MTBF=100h, MTTR=10h. Availability =

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Systematic Layout Planning (SLP):
options: Muther's procedure. Relationship chart (A, E, I, O, U, X). Space requirements. Practical constraints
questions.solution: SLP: P-Q analysis. From-To chart. Relationship (closeness) diagram. Space relationship diagram.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Forecast error measures:
options: MAD = Σ|e|/n, MSE = Σe²/n, MAPE = Σ|e/D|/n × 100
questions.solution: MAD: mean absolute deviation. MSE: penalizes large errors. MAPE: percentage error.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Safety stock: SS = z × σ_L. For 95% service level, σ_L = 20:
questions.solution: z: 95%→1.645, 99%→2.33. σ_L = √(L×σ_d² + d²×σ_L²). Safety stock protects against variability.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: On the inventory cost curve shown (D = 2000, S = ₹40, H = ₹5), the order size Q* that minimizes total cost (where ordering and holding costs are equal) is:
questions.solution: Total cost is minimum at the EOQ Q* = √(2DS/H) = √(2×2000×40/5) ≈ 178.9 units, where the two cost curves cross.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: An activity's normal time/cost are 8 days/₹4000 and crash time/cost are 5 days/₹5000. Its crash cost slope is:
questions.solution: Cost slope = (crash − normal cost)/(normal − crash time) = (5000−4000)/(8−5) = ₹333.3/day.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Lot sizing in MRP — Wagner-Whitin:
options: Dynamic programming. Optimal lot sizes with varying demand. Minimizes setup + holding. More accurate than EOQ
questions.solution: Wagner-Whitin: O(T²) or O(T) algorithm. No constant demand assumption. Least total cost.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Graphical method:
options: Two variables. Plot constraints, find feasible region, optimal at extreme point
questions.solution: Graphical: iso-profit/iso-cost line. Corner point method: evaluate objective at each vertex.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Reliability-Centered Maintenance (RCM):
options: Failure mode analysis → maintenance task selection. 7 questions: functions, failures, effects, consequences, tasks
questions.solution: RCM: MSG-3 (aviation). Hidden vs evident failures. Safety, environmental, operational, non-operational.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Linear programming: feasible region is always:
questions.solution: LP constraints are linear inequalities. Intersection of half-spaces is always convex.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Predictive maintenance — thermography:
options: Infrared camera. Hot spots: electrical faults, friction, insulation breakdown
questions.solution: Thermography: temperature patterns. ΔT >10°C → investigate. Reflected temperature correction.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: MODI (Modified Distribution) method:
options: Optimality check for transportation. u_i + v_j = c_{ij} for basic variables
questions.solution: MODI: compute u, v from basic cells. Δ = c_{ij} - (u_i+v_j) for non-basic. Negative Δ → improve.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Transportation problem: a basic feasible solution for m sources × n destinations has at most:
questions.solution: Transportation: m+n-1 basic cells. Non-basic = m×n-(m+n-1) cells at zero.

questions.stem: Series reliability: R₁=0.9, R₂=0.9. R_series=

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: CPM crashing:
options: Reduce duration at added cost. Optimize cost-time. Crashing critical activities first
questions.solution: Crash cost = (crash cost - normal cost)/(normal time - crash time). Cost slope.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Motor current analysis (MCSA):
options: Identify rotor bar defects, eccentricity, bearing faults from current spectrum. Sidebands around 60 Hz
questions.solution: MCSA: non-invasive. Rotor bar: (1±2s) f_0 sidebands. Eccentricity: (f_0 ± f_r) sidebands.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Critical path:
options: Longest path through network. Zero float activities. Determines project duration
questions.solution: Critical path: any delay → project delay. May have multiple critical paths.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Bullwhip effect causes:
options: Demand signal processing, order batching, price fluctuation, rationing
questions.solution: Lee et al.: 4 causes. Mitigation: EDI, VMI, ECR, continuous replenishment.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Burned vs stable requirements — PERT/CPM vs Agile:
options: PERT: plan-driven, sequential. Agile: iterative, adaptive. Hybrid: plan the work, work the plan (adapted)
questions.solution: Hybrid: plan at high level, iterate at task level. Rolling wave planning. 3-point estimates + velocity.

questions.subject: Industrial Engineering
questions.stem: Sensitivity analysis in LP examines:
options: Effect of changing parameters on optimal solution
questions.solution: Shadow price = rate of change of Z with RHS.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Chicken-egg problem in SC — long-term contract:
options: Fixed price protects buyer when prices rise. Index-based (linked to commodity index) for fairness
questions.solution: Long-term: relationship-specific investment. Transaction cost economics. Hold-up problem.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Oil analysis — atomic emission:
options: ppm wear metals (Fe, Cu, Al, Cr) + additive depletion (Zn, Ca, Mg, P). RDE (rotating disc electrode)
questions.solution: Oil: wear particle analysis. PQ index (particle quantifier). FTIR: oxidation, nitration, water.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: For D = 4900/yr, S = ₹80, H = ₹4/unit/yr, the minimum total ordering-plus-holding cost (at EOQ ≈ 442.7) is about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A transportation problem seeks to minimize cost of shipping from sources to destinations subject to:
questions.solution: It balances supplies at origins against demands at destinations at least total cost.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Pareto analysis:
options: 80% of problems come from 20% of causes. Focus on vital few
questions.solution: Pareto: ranked bar chart + cumulative line. Juran's principle. Focus on top causes.

questions.subject: Industrial Engineering
questions.stem: Naive forecast method assumes:
options: Next period equals most recent actual

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Moving average:
options: Average of last k periods. Smooths random variation. Lags trend
questions.solution: MA(3): (D_t + D_{t-1} + D_{t-2})/3. Larger k = more smoothing. Lag: k/2 periods.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: With α = 0.3, last actual = 250 and last forecast = 220, the next exponential-smoothing forecast is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Control chart: upper control limit (UCL) for X-bar chart:
questions.solution: X-bar chart: UCL = X̄ + A₂R̄. A₂ depends on sample size (n). A₂ for n=5 is 0.577.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Control chart — EWMA:
options: Exponentially weighted moving average. λ = weight. Detects small shifts
questions.solution: EWMA: z_t = λ·x̄_t + (1-λ)·z_{t-1}. λ (0.05-0.2). Small shift detection better than Shewhart.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Total Quality Management (TQM):
options: Organization-wide commitment to continuous improvement. Customer focus. Deming, Juran, Crosby
questions.solution: TQM: employee involvement, process improvement, customer satisfaction. Zero defects.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A product is used at 30 units/day with a lead time of 10 days. The reorder point (no safety stock) is:
questions.solution: ROP = demand rate × lead time = 30×10 = 300 units.

questions.subject: Industrial Engineering
questions.stem: RULA (Rapid Upper Limb Assessment) score of 5-6 indicates:
questions.solution: RULA: 1-2 acceptable, 3-4 investigate, 5-6 change soon, 7+ change immediately. Upper limb disorder risk.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Group Technology — coding:
options: Optiz (5 digits: shape, form elements, dimension, material, accuracy). For part family identification
questions.solution: Optiz: hierarchical. 1st digit: part class (rotational, non-rotational). 2nd: form elements. 3rd: dimension. 4th: material. 5th: accuracy.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Make-to-stock (MTS):
options: Produce before demand. FG inventory. Short delivery. Forecasting essential
questions.solution: MTS: high volume, standard products. Risk of obsolescence. Forecast accuracy critical.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: CORELAP (Computerized Relationship Layout):
options: Constructive. Total closeness rating (TCR). Highest TCR placed first
questions.solution: CORELAP: TCR = Σ closeness ratings. Place to maximize adjacency score. Rectilinear.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Kaizen:
options: Continuous incremental improvement. Small changes, everyone involved
questions.solution: Kaizen: 5S (Sort, Set, Shine, Standardize, Sustain). Poka-yoke (mistake proofing).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Standard time calculation: ST = NT * (1 + allowance). If allowance is 20%:
questions.solution: Allowance: added to normal time. 20% allowance: ST = NT * 1.20. Personal, fatigue, delay.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Customers arrive at 15/hour and spend on average 0.75 hour in the system. The average number in the system is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: SDE (Scarce-Difficult-Easy):
options: By availability/lead time. S: scarce (imported). D: difficult. E: easy (local)
questions.solution: SDE: for procurement. S: long lead time, advance purchase. E: local, short lead time.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Annual demand = 5000 units, ordering cost = ₹50/order, holding cost = ₹2/unit/yr. The EOQ is about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: VE — benchmarking:
options: Compare with best-in-class. Metrics: cost, quality, time, features. Identify improvement gap
questions.solution: Benchmarking: competitive, functional, generic. Reverse engineering. Best practices transfer.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Annual demand = 1000 units, ordering cost = ₹50/order, holding cost = ₹5/unit/yr. The EOQ is about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Seasonal decomposition: multiplicative model:
options: Y = T * S * C * I (Trend x Seasonal x Cyclical x Irregular)
questions.solution: Multiplicative: seasonal component proportional to trend. Additive: seasonal constant regardless of trend.

questions.subject: Industrial Engineering
questions.stem: Systematic Layout Planning (SLP) uses:
questions.solution: SLP: Muther. REL chart (A-E-I-O-U-X). Activity relationships drive layout. Iterative process.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Depreciation methods:
options: Straight line (SL), declining balance (DB), sum-of-years-digits (SYD), MACRS
questions.solution: SL: equal annual. DB: accelerated. MACRS: IRS guidelines for tax. Salvage value.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Markov — absorbing state:
options: P_ii = 1 (once entered, never left). Fundamental matrix: (I-Q)^(-1)
questions.solution: Absorbing: eventual absorption probability. Expected time to absorption. Mean first passage time.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: OHSAS 18001 / ISO 45001:
options: Occupational health and safety management. Risk-based approach
questions.solution: ISO 45001: hazard identification, risk assessment, control hierarchy (elimination > PPE).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Loading:
options: Assigning work to work centers (machines/people). Machine loading chart
questions.solution: Loading: capacity check. Infinite loading (ignore capacity) vs finite loading (consider capacity).
